meaning: adj.不露情感的,没有情感的
examples: 60-second theatre with flat, emotionless, synthesized voices makes this a minimalist art form.
He sat emotionless in the pew for the remainder of the service, waiting for the congregation to exit.
Many people make a negative impression on video by becoming stiff and emotionless, or by exaggerating their points.
His wife, played by Amy Adams, sits at his side, looking on nearly emotionless.
Evert was emotionless on the court, gutting weaker-willed opponents with her adamantine demeanor.
